mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future equity cash flows, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or positive returns occur in > 55% of rising rate periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals produce negative 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output that triggers short-term risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered observations during tightening regimes, or signal loses predictive power when volatility exceeds 20%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising consumer price inflation and stable federal funds rates creates a real rate compression that fuels equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day returns turn negative in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect reverses sign in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumption capacity, which directly supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive payroll growth correlates with negative 20-day returns in > 50% of months.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly drop in real GDP growth below 1.0% annualized signals demand contraction that forces earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered quarters, or the signal fails to predict direction during high liquidity environments.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates supply-side tightening that boosts corporate pricing power and profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.
